journal_entry.description: Employee meal benefit for overtime workers paid via corporate credit card
journal_entry.rationale: Meals provided to employees working overtime as an employee benefit are recorded as employee benefits expense (or meals and entertainment expense). Under IFRS, this is an operating expense. The corporate credit card creates a liability (credit card payable). The transaction text clearly states this is an employee benefit for overtime workers, so the business purpose is unambiguous. No tax is applicable as this is a meal expense paid by the company (input tax credits on meals are typically restricted to 50% in Canada, but the gross amount is still expensed and any recoverable portion would be handled separately).

output.reason: The meal provided to employees working overtime as an employee benefit is an employee benefit expense. This represents an expense increase (debit to expense account). The payment method (corporate credit card) affects the credit side only, not the debit classification.

debit_classifier.reason: The meal provided to employees working overtime as an employee benefit is an employee benefit expense. This represents an expense increase (debit to expense account). The payment method (corporate credit card) affects the credit side only, not the debit classification.

output.reason: Corporate credit card used = liability increase. When the company uses a corporate credit card to pay for the meal, it creates a credit card payable (liability increase). The debit side would record the employee benefit expense, but we only classify the credit side, which is the credit card liability.

credit_classifier.reason: Corporate credit card used = liability increase. When the company uses a corporate credit card to pay for the meal, it creates a credit card payable (liability increase). The debit side would record the employee benefit expense, but we only classify the credit side, which is the credit card liability.

output.reason: The transaction describes a meal provided to employees working overtime as an employee benefit, paid via corporate credit card. This is an employee benefit expense (compensation-related expense under IAS 19). The debit side correctly records an expense increase (c=1). The credit side shows a liability increase (a=1), which is consistent - using a corporate credit card creates a liability (credit card payable). The two sides tell a consistent story: debit expense, credit liability. No correction needed.

debit_corrector.reason: The transaction describes a meal provided to employees working overtime as an employee benefit, paid via corporate credit card. This is an employee benefit expense (compensation-related expense under IAS 19). The debit side correctly records an expense increase (c=1). The credit side shows a liability increase (a=1), which is consistent - using a corporate credit card creates a liability (credit card payable). The two sides tell a consistent story: debit expense, credit liability. No correction needed.

output.reason: The transaction describes a meal paid using a corporate credit card. This creates two accounting effects: (1) an expense is recognized for the employee benefit (debit to expense), and (2) a liability is created for the amount owed on the corporate credit card (credit to liability - accounts payable/credit card payable). The debit tuple correctly shows expense increase (slot c=1). The initial credit tuple correctly shows liability increase (slot a=1) representing the credit card payable. The two sides tell a consistent story: expense up (debit) and liability up (credit). No correction needed.

This creates two accounting effects: (1) an expense is recognized for the employee benefit (debit to expense), and (2) a liability is created for the amount owed on the corporate credit card (credit to liability - accounts payable/credit card payable). The debit tuple correctly shows expense increase (slot c=1). The initial credit tuple correctly shows liability increase (slot a=1) representing the credit card payable. The two sides tell a consistent story: expense up (debit) and liability up (credit). No correction needed.
